description: A slow-moving lizard known for its remarkable color-changing ability. Found in northern Tunisia in shrubby coastal areas and gardens.
habitat: Coastal Mediterranean scrubland, gardens, and dry open forests in northern Tunisia.
ecologicalRole: Controls insect populations through predation. Acts as prey for larger raptors and snakes.
threats: Habitat loss due to urbanization, illegal pet trade, and use of pesticides.

description: A medium-sized falcon closely related to the Peregrine Falcon. Known for its speed and agility in flight.
habitat: Rocky cliffs, coastal areas, and desert edges across North Africa.
ecologicalRole: Apex predator controlling populations of pigeons and small birds.
threats: Nest disturbance, hunting, and reduction in prey due to pesticide use.

description: A venomous viper adapted to sandy desert environments. Recognized by the small horn above each eye.
habitat: Sandy and gravelly deserts of the Sahara and surrounding areas.
ecologicalRole: Controls rodent populations. Important prey for eagles and monitor lizards.
threats: Persecution by humans, illegal wildlife trade, and habitat degradation.

description: A resilient pine tree native to the Mediterranean region. Widely planted in Tunisia for reforestation.
habitat: Rocky hillsides, dry Mediterranean forests, and coastal areas.
ecologicalRole: Provides habitat and food for birds and insects. Stabilizes rocky soils and prevents erosion.
threats: Forest fires, climate change, and bark beetle infestations.

description: A critically threatened gazelle of the Saharan region. Extremely fast and adapted to sandy terrain.
habitat: Flat sandy deserts and open sandy plains of the Sahara.
ecologicalRole: Key herbivore maintaining desert vegetation structure. Prey for cheetahs and wolves.
threats: Illegal hunting, habitat encroachment, and competition with livestock.

description: A flowering plant common across Tunisian hillsides. Blooms white or pink in spring.
habitat: Garrigue, rocky hillsides, olive groves, and open grasslands.
ecologicalRole: Provides nectar for pollinators. Pioneer species after fires, helping ecosystems recover.
threats: Overgrazing by livestock and invasive plant species.

description: A majestic conifer native to the Atlas Mountains in North Africa. Can live for centuries.
habitat: Mountainous forests of Aïn Draham and the Kroumirie region in northwest Tunisia.
ecologicalRole: Supports rich biodiversity including rare birds and mammals. Regulates water cycles.
threats: Climate change-driven drought, unsustainable logging, and overgrazing.
